computation_name: Sequence Seed Prefilter with Alignment Reranking
definition: Use exact or spaced seeds, k-mers, or minimizers to generate likely homologous train candidates, then compute the intended alignment, edit-distance, or identity score only for those candidates.

scale_profile.best_for: Sequence datasets where alignments are expensive but seed overlap is a reliable prefilter.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of sequences with suitable seed indexing and length filters.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Remote homology or low-complexity regions make seed sensitivity unreliable without calibration.

tunable_parameters.name: seed_pattern
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls sensitivity and selectivity of candidate retrieval.

tunable_parameters.name: kmer_size
tunable_parameters.purpose: Length of exact seed or k-mer tokens.

quality_gates: Estimate seed sensitivity on known similar pairs or an exact subset.
Report sequence filtering, low-complexity handling, and alignment parameters.
Confirm that hard SPECTRA bins do not simply contain sequences missed by the prefilter.
failure_modes: Seed filters can miss remote or gapped relationships.
Low-complexity sequences can create excessive candidate lists.
Length-normalization choices can change the similarity interpretation.
example_usage: Tokenize sequences into k-mers, run minhash_lsh_jaccard.py for candidates, then replace the final score with sequence identity or alignment similarity when needed.

citations.title: Improved seed design for protein similarity search
citations.evidence: Discusses seed-based protein similarity search, sensitivity/selectivity tradeoffs, and reducing hash-table accesses.

citations.title: SubseqHash2: highly-sensitive seed-based sequence similarity search
citations.evidence: Uses seed-based candidate generation for sequence similarity search on error-prone sequences.
